name: Barbecue Sauce (Chef Paul Prudhomme)
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Pork, beef or chicken stock
¾ cup Dry roasted pecans, chopped
Juice and rind from ½ orange
Juice and rind from ¼ lemon
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl and set aside.
2. In a 2-quart saucepan, fry the bacon over high heat until crisp.
3. Stir in onions, cover the pan, and continue cooking until onions are dark brown, about 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Stir in the seasoning mix and cook for about 1 minute.
5. Add the stock, chili sauce, honey, pecans, orange juice, lemon juice, orange rind and pulp, lemon rind and pulp, garlic, and Tabasco, stirring well.
6. Reduce heat to low and continue cooking for about 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
7. Remove the orange and lemon rinds.
8. Continue cooking and stirring for another 15 minutes to let the flavors marry.
9. Add the butter and stir until melted.
10. Remove from heat and let cool for about 30 minutes before transferring to a food processor or blender. Process until pecans and bacon are finely chopped.
11. This sauce may be used to barbecue chicken, pork, or ribs. Makes about 5 cups.
